title: Created an Excel Spreadsheet That Tracks Errors
description: There was an ongoing issue with charges being missed on our trucks, so I created an Excel spreadsheet to track all of them accurately. During this project I learned how to use a wide range of formulas, allowing the sheet to automate most of the work instead of requiring manual entry. Now, all I need to do is type in the order number, user name, and charge type with the count — the rest auto-populates. When the order number is entered, the sheet reads the left digit to determine whether the order is inbound or outbound. Entering the user name automatically pulls the full name from a separate reference sheet. After selecting the charge type, the spreadsheet identifies whether the charge was inbound or outbound and whether it was missed. It then compiles all of this information to generate a clean, organized table showing the user, charge count, and type of error.

title: Used Error Tracking Sheet to Create a Cleaner Report Style Sheet
description: I wanted to create a cleaner and more accessible way for everyone to view the charge errors, so I built a second Excel sheet that visually summarizes the data. This sheet automatically pulls information from the main error-tracking workbook for both the current week and the previous week. After refreshing the pivot table on the main page, the sheet updates, displaying the total number of errors per person. It also adds the weekly total of all individuals and applies color-coded formatting to indicate whether the total above or below the previous week.

title: Remade the Warehouse Map
description: To reduce unnecessary travel time for warehouse personnel, I redesigned our warehouse map from the ground up. I began by analyzing the frequency and volume of items received from each customer, then carefully audited every product, both systematically and physically to confirm each item was assigned to the correct customer. I also calculated the total pallet count and projected volume for each customer. Using this data, I restructured the entire warehouse layout to ensure that all products were placed in locations that made logical sense and significantly reduced travel distance. I also centralized several quick turnaround items, making it faster and more efficient for warehousemen to move pallets and complete their tasks.
